speaker: Attorney Grzyb
description: Voice-reference dialogue samples for Attorney Grzyb in Pig & Swine RPG. These are draft samples, not final committed game text.
source_role_summary: Procedural-regularity witness against the mass closure scheme

voice_profile.core_voice: pompous, precise, beige, procedural, faintly fungal, funny but not stupid
comedy_engine: uses dry formal language for absurdly overbroad control
believes rights should be exercised with restraint, paperwork, and preferably beige
is outraged by procedural irregularity when it offends his own formalism
becomes useful in Chapter 5 because his love of rules is sincere
do: make him a plausible opponent, not an idiot
let him be funny through over-formality and beige obsession
make his defeats precise and indignant
in Chapter 5, let him object to the mass scheme because it is procedurally rotten
do_not: make him secretly kind in Chapter 2
make every line a mushroom joke
make him use modern villain monologues

relationship_dynamics.Dr. A. Cula: underestimates him as a sentimental novice, then grudgingly respects his preparation
relationship_dynamics.Waldek: frames him as a tenant in breach, not as a person; later forced to confront the human consequence
relationship_dynamics.Kowalski: finds him irritating but difficult to dismiss because he is evidentially precise
relationship_dynamics.Zielinska: dismisses her as partial, then struggles with her practical clarity
relationship_dynamics.Mr. Murrow: recognises him as a serious procedural mind from the opposing side
relationship_dynamics.Crab: dislikes Crab's factual cuts because they puncture formal presentation
relationship_dynamics.Whimsy: finds him theatrical in the wrong colour palette
relationship_dynamics.Advocate Szpon: dislikes Szpon's procedural opportunism in Chapter 5
relationship_dynamics.Mr. Pig: regards him as unserious, then slowly sees why the office matters

notes: Every line is a voice-reference draft for later rewriting into godot/data/dialogues/dialogues.json.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: Civilisation begins when people agree not to treat every window as a manifesto.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: My client does not oppose rights. My client merely insists that rights be exercised in beige.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: Procedure is not oppression simply because it has margins.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: The word disproportionate is often used by persons who have misplaced their obligations.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: A building is not a noticeboard for every private weather pattern.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: Rules are the grammar of shared life. Without grammar, one has shouting and wallpaper.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: I am not against expression. I am against expression that arrives without permission and expects applause.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: If every tenant improvises, management becomes archaeology.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: The law protects homes. It does not convert every lease into a constitutional stage.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: Order is not fashionable, counsel. It is necessary.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: A vague accusation is improper. A vague defence is not improved by righteous lighting.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: My submissions are not beige. They are restrained.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: I object to the suggestion that formalism lacks compassion. Formalism prevents neighbours from becoming weather.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: One may sympathise with a person and still require compliance.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: Rights are serious things. That is why they should be filed correctly.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: I prefer arriving early. It gives the other side time to become visibly underprepared.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: The benches are uncomfortable, but they preserve a necessary seriousness.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: I see Pig & Swine has brought enthusiasm. I hope it has also brought evidence.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: Court corridors are useful. They reveal which counsel respects folders.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: A rolled poster in court always suggests someone has confused advocacy with interior design.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: If counsel intends to rely on proportionality, I trust counsel has found some law beneath the adjectives.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: The judge dislikes surprises. I share the sentiment, except when causing them.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: Please do not block the corridor. Procedural rights include passage.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: I have never trusted a file held together by optimism.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: If this matter settles, I shall be relieved professionally and disappointed aesthetically.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: A rule may be imperfect and still be binding.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: Tenants frequently discover principles after receiving notices.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: Landlords are not villains merely because they own stationery.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: The façade is part of the common environment. That is not poetry; it is property management.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: People mock beige until they require neutrality.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: One must distinguish hardship from legal excuse.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: I do not enjoy conflict. I enjoy order, and conflict often interrupts it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: If I sound severe, it is because vague compassion produces untidy results.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: A right exercised without regard to others becomes noise.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: The law is rarely improved by theatrical scarves.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: Counsel, do not romanticise tenancy obligations. Civilisation depends on rules, and rules depend on beige.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: I represent the landlord in this regrettable matter of window-based escalation.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: Your client has converted a simple compliance issue into a miniature constitutional parade.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: The building rules are not decorative. Unlike, apparently, several windows in that courtyard.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: Management is entitled to preserve order, harmony, and the ordinary dignity of shared premises.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: A lease is not a poem. It imposes obligations.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: If every tenant asserts expressive autonomy through glass, the building becomes a pamphlet with plumbing.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: I advise your firm not to mistake sympathy for a defence.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: The rule was available. The warnings were served. The tenant persisted.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: This matter should not require litigation. It requires removal of a poster and a little maturity.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: Dr. A. Cula, a word of professional courtesy: housing law is not improved by sentiment.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: The court will not ask whether the poster is charming. It will ask whether the tenant complied.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: My client gave warnings. Your client chose performance.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: Do not build your case on the silence of neighbours. People often suffer quietly.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: Do not build it on proportionality either, unless you enjoy balancing clouds.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: The tenant accepted building rules. Regret does not amend a lease.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: A rule need not list every possible misuse of a window to be enforceable.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: Management discretion exists because buildings generate more nonsense than contracts can enumerate.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: I have seen many first-day moral crusades. They rarely survive the evidence bundle.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: If Pig & Swine wishes to become respectable, it should begin by distinguishing a hard case from a good one.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: Mr. Pig appears to be captaining a financial incident.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: Mr. Murrow I recognise as structurally dangerous. The rest of you are colourful.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: Crab has the manner of a man who trusts postmarks more than people. Occasionally useful, I admit.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: Whimsy should not be allowed near headings.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: Asia is the only person in your office who appears to understand filing. I say this with professional admiration.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: Dr. A. Cula, you seem sincere. Courts are kind to sincerity only after evidence has done the work.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: Pig & Swine's office has charm in the way collapsing shelves have charm.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: A law firm should not look like a lost-and-found box for procedural anxiety.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: Still, I concede you have acquired a client, a theory, and a poster. An eccentric trinity.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: If you intend to oppose me, please at least paginate.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: Mr. Waldek is not being evicted for having thoughts. He is being evicted for breaching agreed rules.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: One may be sincere and still non-compliant.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: Your client received warnings and chose continued display.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: A tenant cannot transform every management instruction into a referendum on dignity.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: If Mr. Waldek wished to challenge the rule, he had orderly means available.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: Instead, he maintained the breach and now asks the court to constitutionalise the consequence.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: The home is important. That is precisely why shared living requires predictable rules.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: My client did not object to a person. My client objected to visible non-compliance.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: The poster may be quiet, but defiance is not always acoustic.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: Mr. Waldek's hardship is regrettable. It is not automatically a defence.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: The rule must be read in context: a shared residential building, common visual environment, repeated warnings.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: Façade harmony is not mystical. It is the ordinary expectation that shared property not become private exhibition space.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: A rule can use general language and remain enforceable. Life is too inventive for exhaustive lists.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: Management discretion is not lawlessness. It is the tool by which buildings remain habitable.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: Visual agitation is an inelegant phrase, perhaps, but inelegance is not invalidity.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: The tenant knew what was being asked of him: remove the poster.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: The rule was not invented in court. It was part of the management framework.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: If a tenant disagrees, the answer is dialogue, not persistence until termination becomes unavoidable.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: The court should not require landlords to litigate every adjective before managing a building.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: A broad rule is sometimes necessary where conduct takes many forms.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: Residents are not required to expose themselves to neighbour conflict before management acts.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: Complaints may be confidential. That does not make them fictional.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: A building's atmosphere is not measured only in decibels.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: Disturbance includes tension, discomfort, and the visible disregard of shared standards.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: My client was entitled to consider cumulative concern.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: The complaint record confirms management did not act on whim.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: It is easy to mock a file until one remembers that files exist because people complain quietly.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: The absence of a neighbour shouting in the hallway is not proof of harmony.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: The landlord need not wait for a riot of curtains before enforcing rules.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: If some pages are imperfect, that does not erase the management concern.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: Eviction is serious. So is persistent refusal to comply with lawful obligations.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: Proportionality does not mean every consequence must be gentle.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: The landlord issued warnings before termination. The tenant chose not to correct the breach.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: Lesser measures were attempted through warnings. They failed.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: The court must balance the tenant's interest against the rights of other residents and the owner's management duties.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: A home does not exempt a tenant from conditions accepted under the lease.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: The expressive element does not immunise the conduct.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: A proportionate system must also protect the quiet expectations of others.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: If the court rewards refusal, building rules become optional suggestions.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: The remedy sought by the tenant would leave management with a rule it cannot enforce.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: That file is not fleeing. It is being mishandled.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: Counsel, please refrain from converting document review into a stamping carnival.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: Those pages are confidential management records, not migratory birds.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: This is precisely why files should not be opened dramatically near windows.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: I object to the implication that wind is cross-examination.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: Recover the pages carefully. Preferably without theatrical shouting.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: If Whimsy says bureaucratic wildlife, I will leave.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: A complaint file in motion is still a complaint file.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: Please do not stamp irrelevant paper. The evidential dignity of this room is already wounded.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: I insist that any recovered page be treated in context, not triumph.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: The dates require explanation. They do not, however, destroy the entire management position.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: A late notation may reflect late recording, not late concern.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: I would caution against treating clerical imperfection as conspiracy.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: The complaint page is less elegant than I had been led to expect.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: Duplications can occur in active management files.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: The chronology is... not ideal.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: I will need instructions on that date.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: Counsel should not overstate the point. Though I accept the point now exists.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: A record may be imperfect and still relevant. This one is unfortunately very imperfect.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: The court will hear submissions on weight, not theatrics.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: A torn document is a poor foundation for accusation.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: The page is damaged. I trust counsel will not ask it to carry more than it can.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: Even if the date is legible, the context is missing.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: This is what happens when evidence is chased rather than reviewed.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: My client should not be prejudiced by wind.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: The court should be cautious with fragments.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: That page may raise a question. It does not answer the case.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: I object to dramatic confidence based on half a document.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: If the record is incomplete, the proper response is caution, not celebration.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: Still, I accept the date should be addressed.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: Then we return to the actual record rather than the imagined one.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: A failed paper chase is not evidence.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: Counsel cannot rely on documents that have joined the atmosphere.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: The complaint record should not be replaced by regret.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: Witnesses may speculate. Files, when retained, are better.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: I will resist any attempt to cross-examine the wind.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: This unfortunate episode proves only that the room is inadequately ventilated.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: My client's case remains supported by warnings and management concern.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: If the defence required those pages, that is not my client's burden.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: Now, perhaps, we can return to law.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: Mr. Kowalski's enthusiasm for observation is noted.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: A neighbour's failure to complain is not proof that no resident was disturbed.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: Mr. Kowalski appears to confuse personal surveillance with collective knowledge.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: His evidence may be colourful. Colour is not always weight.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: The court should be cautious before treating one man's curtains as a monitoring system.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: He complains often. That does not make silence dispositive.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: I accept he noticed the poster. I do not accept he represents the building.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: A witness who hears everything may also hear what he expects.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: His remarks about typography are irrelevant, though regrettably memorable.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: The landlord was not required to consult Mr. Kowalski before acting.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: Ms. Zielinska is plainly sincere. Sincerity, again, is not the legal test.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: Selective enforcement is a serious allegation. It requires more than neighbourly impression.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: Other decorations may differ in content, size, placement, or context.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: Management is entitled to distinguish between harmless decoration and contentious display.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: The court should not convert every difference in enforcement into discrimination.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: Her evidence describes perception. My client acted on management responsibility.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: I do not dispute that she cares about Mr. Waldek. I dispute the inference she draws.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: A practical witness can still be partial.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: The landlord's discretion includes judgment. Judgment is not automatically targeting.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: The fact that she is calm does not make her conclusion inevitable.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: Pig & Swine has arranged its chaos into something resembling a case.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: I see the defence has discovered structure. Troubling, but welcome.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: The mapping is neat. I will attack the assumptions beneath it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: A board with string does not become law, but this one is less decorative than expected.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: Counsel appears to have separated notice, rule, fact, and remedy. Sensible.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: Preparation is not persuasion, though it improves the theatre of it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: The court may enjoy the clarity. I will provide the necessary resistance.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: I had hoped the poster would remain the defence's strongest visual aid.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: Very well. We shall argue the issues rather than the furniture.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: I concede nothing except that you have located your documents.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: Your Honour, this is not a case about censorship. It is a case about a tenant's persistent breach of agreed building rules.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: The landlord acted after warnings, after continued non-compliance, and within the management framework.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: The defence seeks to transform a simple obligation into a constitutional drama.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: A shared residential building requires rules capable of preserving order for all residents.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: Mr. Waldek was not evicted for a belief. He was subject to termination for refusing a lawful instruction.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: The court should resist the invitation to make every window a forum immune from management.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: Hardship is regrettable. It does not erase obligation.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: The landlord's discretion exists precisely for situations where conduct disrupts the common environment.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: The tenant had choices. He chose persistence.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Attorney Grzyb
text: The notice followed a pattern of warnings, concern, and refusal.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.
